The flour remains under my fingernails, a pale crescent of dust marking the weight of my resolve
The sour yeast on my forearms and the neon liquor store blurred into a soft, bruised purple smudge
The scent of yeast on my forearms, honest and slow, unlike the city’s sharp, metallic tang
The scent of yeast on my forearms, honest and slow, against the sharp, metallic tang of the city
The cold iron of the railing bites into my palm, more real than the flickering neon signs
